title: Rust autograd LayerNorm and RoPE backward ops
description:
Implement two new autograd backward operations:

1. `synapse/crates/synapse-autograd/src/ops/layernorm.rs`: LayerNormBackward implementing GradFn.
   - Saves: input, mean, rstd (reciprocal std), weight
   - Returns: [grad_input, grad_weight, grad_bias]
   - Forward calls FFI syn_layernorm_forward

2. `synapse/crates/synapse-autograd/src/ops/rope.rs`: RoPEBackward implementing GradFn.
   - RoPE backward is the inverse rotation (negate sin component):
     ```
     grad_input[..., 2i]   = grad[..., 2i] * cos[pos,i] + grad[..., 2i+1] * sin[pos,i]
     grad_input[..., 2i+1] = -grad[..., 2i] * sin[pos,i] + grad[..., 2i+1] * cos[pos,i]
     ```
   - Saves: cos_table, sin_table
   - Returns: [grad_input]

Register both new modules in ops mod.rs (add `pub mod layernorm;` and `pub mod rope;`).

Tests:
- grad_check for LayerNorm: shapes [32, 64], [8, 16, 32]. Verify grad_input, grad_weight, grad_bias all pass.
- grad_check for RoPE: shapes [2, 4, 16, 32]. Verify grad_input passes.
- Edge cases: constant input to LayerNorm (near-zero variance) — no inf/nan.

Pass/fail:
- grad_check passes for all LayerNorm gradients (relative error < 1e-3)
- grad_check passes for RoPE gradient (relative error < 1e-3)
- Handles edge cases without inf/nan
